text.title: Grafana Deployment Rules
text.summary: Rules for choosing a deployment model (Docker Compose vs Helm vs HA), the Grafana Agent EOL, and database backend requirements for HA.

rule.statement: Use Docker Compose for development and single-node deployments; Kubernetes Helm for production; HA with PostgreSQL for critical systems.
rule.rationale: SQLite is not supported in HA configurations — any multi-replica setup requires PostgreSQL or MySQL as the shared database.

rule.statement: Use Grafana Alloy as the data collection agent — Grafana Agent is EOL since November 1, 2025.
rule.rationale: Grafana Agent (Static, Flow, and Operator modes) reached end-of-life on November 1, 2025. All three modes migrate to Grafana Alloy via the `alloy convert` command.

rule.statement: For HA: deploy PostgreSQL or MySQL as the shared database, expose a headless Kubernetes Service for alerting peer discovery, and set min 2 replicas behind a load balancer.
rule.rationale: Sessions are stored in the database, so no session affinity is needed on the load balancer. Alerting HA uses gossip via `ha_listen_address` and `ha_peers` (or Redis) to deduplicate alert firing.

rule.statement: Configure the health check endpoint `/api/health` on the load balancer, not a TCP check.
rule.rationale: TCP checks pass even when Grafana is starting up. The HTTP health endpoint returns 200 only when Grafana is ready to serve traffic.

rule.statement: Convert existing Grafana Agent configs with `alloy convert --source-format=static --output=config.alloy config.yaml` before EOL, then run Alloy in parallel to validate metrics continuity.
rule.rationale: Parallel-run validation catches label differences or missing scrape targets before the Agent is decommissioned, preventing monitoring gaps.

example.note: Converting a static Agent config to Alloy format.
example.code:
# Static mode conversion alloy convert --source-format=static --output=config.alloy config.yaml
# Flow mode conversion alloy convert --source-format=flow --output=config.alloy config.river

antipattern.description: Deploying multiple Grafana replicas with SQLite.
antipattern.reason: SQLite is file-based and cannot be shared across processes. Concurrent writes from multiple replicas corrupt the database.
